item.title: Asian markets cautious, oil dips after Trump holds off on Iran attack
item.description:
Asian markets were mixed Tuesday as oil prices eased on hopes of a US-Iran deal, though elevated crude levels capped investor appetite for risk.

Energy markets held centre stage after US President Donald Trump signalled "serious negotiations" with Tehran and called off planned strikes, boosting optimism that tensions could.

The war the United States and Israel launched February 28 has led to an effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, through which around 20 percent of global oil exports passed in peacetime.

item.title: What we know about secret Israeli bases in Iraq
item.description: Iraqi authorities have said there are no longer any hostile forces operating in its territory.

item.title: What we know about secret Israeli bases in Iraq
item.description: Iraqi authorities have said there are no longer any hostile forces operating in its territory.

item.title: What we know about secret Israeli bases in Iraq
item.description: Iraqi authorities have said there are no longer any hostile forces operating in its territory.

item.description: Iraqi authorities have said there are no longer any hostile forces operating in its territory.

item.title: Trump says held off on new Iran attack, upbeat for agreement
item.description:
US President Donald Trump said he was planning a major new assault on Iran on Tuesday, but held off as he saw hope for securing a so far elusive agreement to end the war.

Trump said that he stopped his purported attack plan at the urging of Gulf Arab allies, which Iran has threatened with reciprocal attacks if the United States and Israel end a nearly six-week ceasefire.

item.title: Trump says delaying Iran attack at request of Gulf leaders
item.description:
US President Donald Trump said he was postponing a planned attack on Iran scheduled for Tuesday at the request of Gulf leaders amid "serious negotiations" with Tehran.

Trump's sudden announcement on social media on Monday came after Iran said it had responded to a new US proposal aimed at ending the war.

Trump said he would "hold off on our planned Military attack of the Islamic Republic of Iran, which was scheduled for tomorrow," in a message on his Truth Social network.

item.title: War in Middle East: latest developments
item.description:
Here are the latest developments in the Middle East war:

- Trump upbeat on Iran talks -

US President Donald Trump on Monday touted a "very positive development" in talks with Iran, which convinced him to postpone a planned military attack on the Islamic republic.

Trump said allies in the Middle East told him "they are getting very close to making a deal" that would leave Iran without nuclear weapons.

"It's a very positive development, but we'll see whether or not it amounts to anything," Trump said during a White House event.

- Trump postpones Iran attack -

item.title: First attack on Arab nuclear site sends warning to Gulf, US
item.description:
The first attack targeting an Arab nuclear site has sent a symbolic warning to the United Arab Emirates and its allies, even as Iran and the US remain in negotiations to end the Middle East war, analysts say.

An unclaimed drone struck an electrical generator on Sunday near the Arab world's first nuclear power plant in Barakah in the emirate of Abu Dhabi, triggering a fire but causing no injuries nor radiation leak.

item.title: Majority of attacks on Saudi Arabia carried out by pro-Iran militias in Iraq
item.description: Since Feb. 28, most attacks on the kingdom have originated in Iraq and been attributed to Iran-backed Shiite militias, according to regional officials and Iraqi sources.

item.title: Majority of attacks on Saudi Arabia carried out by pro-Iran militias in Iraq
item.description: Since Feb. 28, most attacks on the kingdom have originated in Iraq and been attributed to Iran-backed Shiite militias, according to regional officials and Iraqi sources.

item.title: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver as Iran war squeezes supply
item.description: The waiver temporarily lifts sanctions on Russian oil for 30 days.

item.title: Oil swings as UAE nuclear plant strike, Iran diplomacy push roil markets
item.description: International benchmark Brent crude futures whipsawed on Monday as markets digested a dangerous new escalation alongside signs of diplomatic progress.

item.title: Oil swings as UAE nuclear plant strike, Iran diplomacy push roil markets
item.description: International benchmark Brent crude futures whipsawed on Monday as markets digested a dangerous new escalation alongside signs of diplomatic progress.

item.title: Oil swings as UAE nuclear plant strike, Iran diplomacy push roil markets

item.title: Oil swings as UAE nuclear plant strike, Iran diplomacy push roil markets

item.title: India's Adani to pay $275 mn settlement to US over alleged Iran sanctions violations
item.description:
One of Indian billionaire industrialist Gautam Adani's companies will pay the United States $275 million to settle a probe into whether it violated Washington's sanctions against Iran, the US Treasury said Monday.

The Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) said the agreement had been reached with Adani Enterprises Limited (AEL), part of the magnate's sprawling multinational conglomerate of companies.

item.title: Hormuz tanker traffic edges higher after wartime low
item.description:
Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was slightly higher last week, returning to levels in line with the average recorded since the start of the Middle East conflict after hitting a wartime low.

A total of 55 commodities vessels crossed the strategic waterway between May 11 and 17, according to data from maritime tracking firm Kpler as of Monday morning.
